28.reason: [Should return EOF instead of 0 to correctly signal end-of-file to the caller]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Should return EOF (which is -1) instead of 0, because the caller checks for EOF. Returning 0 will cause the loop to never terminate when the file ends.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Should return EOF (which is -1) instead of 0 to match the loop condition in main that checks for EOF]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Logical error: should return EOF (or c) to correctly terminate the while loop in main, otherwise causes infinite loop.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Should return EOF instead of 0 to correctly signal end-of-file to the caller]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Should return EOF instead of 0 to correctly signal end-of-file to the caller]

43.reason: [Error: This assignment copies the entire struct, but the original reference program copies word and count separately. This is not a logical error per se, but the function insertWord is missing from the function declarations at the top of the file, causing a compilation error.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: This assignment copies the entire struct, but the original reference program copies word and count separately. This is not a logical error per se, but the function insertWord is missing from the function declarations at the top of the file, causing a compilation error.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: This attempts to assign one struct to another, which is not valid in C. Should use strcpy for the word field and assign count separately, as in the reference program.]

64.reason: [Missing prototype for insertWord; called before declaration causes compilation error]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Missing prototype for insertWord; called before declaration causes compilation error]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Missing prototype for insertWord; function used before its definition leads to implicit declaration, which is invalid in C99 and later, causing a compilation error.

20.reason: Declaring loop variable inside for-loop initialization is not allowed in C89/C90 standard, causing a compilation error
